citations_found.ai_summary: The dispute in this case was about a vehicular accident and the claims filed by the victims against the National Insurance Company Ltd. The Gujarat High Court decided on the appeals filed by the insurance company against the judgments and awards passed by the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal, which had ordered the company to pay compensation to the claimants. The court heard all the appeals together as they pertained to the same accident and involved similar facts and issues.

citations_found.ai_summary: The dispute in this case was about the accidental death of Nitya Gopal Kurmi, who allegedly fell from a running train, and the claimant, Kakali Kurmi, sought compensation of Rs. 4,00,000 under the Railways Act. The Railway Claims Tribunal dismissed the claim petition, and the claimant appealed to the Calcutta High Court. The court decided in favor of the claimant, overturning the Tribunal's decision, although the specific details of the court's decision are not provided in the given text.

citations_found.ai_summary: The dispute in this case was about the entitlement of the legal heirs of Ekbir Kumar Barik to claim compensation from The New India Assurance Company Ltd. after his death, which occurred when unknown persons fired at him while he was driving a vehicle. The Calcutta High Court decided in favor of the claimants, overturning the decision of the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal, which had denied compensation on the grounds that the death was not related to the use of the motor vehicle. The court ruled that the claimants were entitled to compensation, although the exact amount is not specified in the provided text.
